exemplar.title: To what extent is the character of Papa Eugene presented as having an entirely negative worldview in Purple Hibiscus by Chimamanda Ngozi Adichie?

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent is the character of Papa Eugene presented as having an entirely negative worldview in Purple Hibiscus by Chimamanda Ngozi Adichie?

subject.title: English A Lang & Lit
subject.description:
**IB English A Language & Literature** is a **Group 1** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **the study of language and literature through a wide variety of texts and media from different periods, styles, and cultures**, with a strong focus on **critical analysis of both literary and non-literary texts, examining how language choices create meaning and effect in different contexts**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Paper 1** | Text analysis | 1h 15m | 2h 15m | 35% (SL), 25% (HL) |
| **Paper 2** | Comparative essay | 1h 45m | 1h 45m | 35% (SL), 25% (HL)  |
| **HL Essay** | Written assignment | N/A | 1200-1500 words | 25% (HL only) |
| **Individual Oral** | Oral presentation | 15 minutes | 15 minutes | 30% (SL), 20% (HL) |

strengths: Clear and sustained focus on the research question throughout the essay
Wellâorganised subâheadings that mirror facets of the question, maintaining alignment
Effective title page and introductory focus establish purpose immediately
weaknesses: Methodology outlined but lacks critical justification of source selection
Occasional digressions into plot summary dilute sharpness of focus
Introduction missing a concise thesis statement directly answering the research question
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The submission demonstrates a coherent, sustained focus on the research question and a logical structure that aligns with the investigationâs aims, meriting performance in the midâtoâupper band. However, the brief âscope of researchâ lacks critical reflection on why particular sources and methods were chosen, and the introduction does not contain a clear, explicit thesis claim. These deficiencies prevent a topâband mark but justify a solid score of 4.

strengths: Good command of primary text with accurate plot references and quotations
Generally correct use of literary and theoretical terminology
Integration of secondary scholarship to support key thematic points
weaknesses: Overreliance on paraphrase with limited synthesis of critical voices
Some inaccuracies in terminology (e.g., mislabeling internal monologue as âdialogueâ)
Critical sources are sometimes of uncertain rigour or duplicated without consolidation
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student shows clear knowledge of the novel and employs subject-specific terminology with reasonable accuracy, placing this work above the lowest band. However, the uneven integration of secondary sources, occasional misuses of technical vocabulary, and superficial source application mean the work does not yet achieve fully effective or consistently accurate use of knowledge and concepts, warranting a score of 3.

strengths: Research is consistently relevant, drawing on a respectable range of critical articles and contextual sources
Analysis is generally clear and supported by well-chosen quotations and primary evidence
The argument is coherent, and the conclusion revisits findings thoughtfully, noting limitations
weaknesses: Evaluation of secondary sources lacks interrogation of authority or perspective
Depth of analysis is sometimes uneven, occasionally summarising rather than dissecting evidence
Counter-arguments and nuanced readings (e.g., Eugeneâs positive actions) are largely absent
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The essay demonstrates solid research quality and relevance, and its thematic analyses are mostly effective, placing it squarely in the mid-range band of the criterion. While individual sections show excellent moments of insight, the overall critical interrogation and balanced evaluation needed for higher marks are only partially met. A mark of 6 reflects this competent but not yet fully sophisticated critical thinking.

strengths: Meets all formal structural requirements (title page, table of contents, clear section headings)
Consistent application of IB formatting conventions in most respects
Structure supports readability and logical progression of argument
weaknesses: Inconsistent citation and bibliography formatting, including raw URLs and duplicate entries
Minor organisational issues in the table of contents (imprecise page ranges)
Paragraph transitions can be formulaic and repetitive
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The presentation is competent and fully functional, satisfying the majority of IB requirements, and thus merits performance in the middle of the band. However, inconsistencies in referencing style and a few layout glitches prevent a top score, making 3 an appropriate evaluation.

strandSelections.reasoning:
The student identifies the primary text and lists a range of secondary sources (academic articles, interviews, online journals) that are generally relevant.  Citations are integrated in-text and in the bibliography, indicating informed selection.

However, the essay offers only a brief, descriptive paragraph on âScope of research,â with little explanation of how or why particular critical voices, theoretical lenses, or methods were chosen.  There is no explicit discussion of analytical tools beyond standard close reading, nor evidence of systematic source evaluation.

Because the methodology is mostly complete but lacks depth of justification and critical reflection on source choice, the work best fits the 3-4 band; the upper value of 4 acknowledges the reasonable breadth of materials used.

The layout includes a formatted title page, consistent use of headings, a functional table of contents, and a complete bibliography.  Font size, spacing, and margins (as visible) appear to respect IB guidelines, and the word count is provided.

However, citation formatting shows inconsistencies (duplicate entries, mixed referencing styles, occasional missing details).  Page numbers are referenced in-text, but it is unclear if all pages of the essay itself are numbered, and some hyperlinks in the bibliography are malformed.

Most requirements are nonetheless met and errors are minor rather than systematic, situating the work in the 3-4 band.  A score of 3 reflects competent but not flawless application of layout and formatting conventions.

The bibliography lists a respectable range of critical and contextual sources, many of which are directly pertinent to Eugeneâs depiction and the novelâs themes.  In-text citations are frequent, and quotations from both primary and secondary materials are used to substantiate claims, signalling purposeful research activity.

Relevance to the research question is generally strong; the chosen studies on silence, patriarchy, colonialism, and religious extremism clearly underpin the essayâs central argument.  A few sources (e.g., web links of uncertain scholarly authority) slightly weaken overall quality, and there is limited evidence of contrasting perspectives or interrogation of source reliability.

Because the research is more than merely adequate but not consistently rigorous or critically appraised, performance aligns with the lower end of the 7-9 âgoodâ band, warranting a score of 7.

The student establishes the topic immediately with a clear, informative title and reiterates it in the opening sentence of the introduction.  The focus on Papa Eugeneâs worldview is maintained throughout the essayâs main sections (Silence, Colonialism, Family, Violence), showing a deliberate organisational link to the research question.

Although there are occasional digressions and some redundancy within paragraphs, the reader is never left in doubt about the topic or its purpose; each section explicitly returns to how Eugeneâs conduct represents a negative worldview.  Transitional phrases (e.g., âAdichie further presentsâ¦â) signal the continuing relevance of each point.

Minor lapsesâsuch as moments where thematic commentary strays into general plot summaryâprevent the communication from reaching sustained excellence, yet overall clarity, sense of purpose, and accurate framing place the work firmly in the 5-6 band.

A coherent argument runs through the essay, culminating in a conclusion that revisits findings and briefly notes limitations.  The student occasionally reflects on the implications of Eugeneâs dual public/private identity and the broader societal critique, indicating some evaluative thinking.

However, the discussion rarely challenges its own stance, and evaluation of secondary perspectives remains surface-level.  The âLimitationsâ paragraph acknowledges gaps but does not explore their significance for the findings.  As a result, the argument, while clear, lacks sustained critical interrogation and nuanced judgement.

The strand therefore fits the mid-range (4-6) band, with a score of 5 to reflect adequate but not consistently critical evaluation.

The essay offers clear thematic analysesâsilence, colonialism, family dynamics, violenceâeach supported with textual evidence and critical commentary.  Quotations are explained, and connections to the research question are usually explicit, resulting in generally persuasive reasoning.

Yet, the analysis can be repetitive, sometimes summarising rather than dissecting evidence.  Claims such as Eugeneâs behaviour âmirrors colonial violenceâ are asserted more than systematically demonstrated, and counter-arguments or nuances (e.g., Eugeneâs charitable actions) are largely absent, limiting depth.

These factors place the work at the top of the 4-6 (adequate) band: conclusions are mostly supported, but the sophistication and balance required for the higher bands are not fully achieved.

The essay meets the required structural elements: title page with research question, table of contents with page numbers, introduction, body sections, conclusion, and bibliography.  Section headings correspond logically to the argumentâs progression, aiding reader navigation.

Paragraphing is mostly coherent, and the ordering of points is clear.  Although some sections are uneven in length and there are occasional abrupt transitions, the overall structure is appropriate and fulfils EE expectations.

Given that all formal requirements are met and the structure supports understanding despite minor organizational roughness, the work merits a score in the highest (3-4) band; a 4 acknowledges full compliance with slight room for refinement.

The student demonstrates solid knowledge of *Purple Hibiscus*, supplying accurate plot references and quotations that support each thematic heading.  Secondary critics are cited to contextualise claims about patriarchy, colonialism, and religion, showing awareness of scholarly discourse.

Nevertheless, some references are duplicated or of uncertain academic rigour, and integration can be more illustrative than analytical.  At times the essay relies heavily on paraphrasing critics rather than synthesising them, indicating partial rather than fully effective application of sources.

Overall understanding is coherent and mostly accurate, but the occasional superficial use of critics keeps performance within the middle (3-4) band at a score of 4.

Subject-specific literary terminologyâmetaphor, personification, motif, juxtapositionâis used frequently and generally correctly.  The student also employs broader conceptual terms such as âpost-colonial,â âauthoritarian,â and âpatriarchyâ with reasonable accuracy.

Minor misuse appears (e.g., calling a first-person narration âdialogueâ) and some analytical language is repetitive, but these slips do not seriously impede meaning.  Technical vocabulary for citation (e.g., âibid.â, page references) is mostly correct, though occasionally inconsistent.

Given the largely accurate and consistent deployment of literary and theoretical terms, balanced against sporadic imprecision, the work merits a 4 within the 3-4 band.

The research question is explicitly stated in the title block and again in the introduction.  It is narrowly focused on the extent to which Papa Eugene is presented as having "an entirely negative worldview," a scope that is appropriate for a single-text literary investigation.

Throughout the body, sub-headings mirror facets of the research question (Silence, Colonialism, Family, Violence), demonstrating an intentional alignment between discussion and question.  Each analytical section attempts to weigh evidence against the qualifier âentirely,â showing purposeful connection.

While the phrasing of the question might have been refined to avoid the absolute âentirely,â it nevertheless guides the essay effectively and is consistently revisited.  This sustained alignment justifies placement in the highest band for this strand.

annotations.comment: The concluding paragraph on silence ties textual and critical evidence back to the research question, reinforcing the essayâs central argument.
The Family section conclusion reiterates themes but does not explicitly revisit the research questionâs qualifier âentirelyââlink back directly to maintain focus.
Correct identification and application of personification and metaphor demonstrate consistent use of literary terminology.
The Violence section opens without framing in a theoretical perspective; introduce a critical framework early to anchor subsequent analysis.
Effective integration of DâAlmeida and Ifechelobi supports the theme of silence as oppression, showing good application of secondary sources.
Repetitive transition phrases (âOn the other hand,â âAnother wayâ) signal formulaic writing; vary connectors to improve narrative flow.
Two entries by Ifechelobi appear duplicated with slight variations; consolidate them to maintain bibliography accuracy.
The bibliography mixes referencing styles and URL formats; apply a single IB-recommended citation style consistently throughout.
Raw URLs display with line breaks and HTML artifacts, disrupting readability; format links per IB guidelines or convert to properly styled references.
The table of contents is clear and logically organized, listing all major sections with accurate page ranges.
Page range labels use ellipses (e.g., â3-5â) which may mislead; ensure section titles in the TOC precisely match actual page start and end numbers.
The introduction provides contextual background but lacks a concise thesis statement directly answering the research question; add an explicit claim to guide the analysis.
The focus paragraph effectively situates Eugeneâs worldview within thematic lenses of fear, silence, and colonialism, demonstrating clear subject understanding.
The âLine of argumentâ header clearly maps out the essay structure, enhancing readability and signaling the organization of subsequent analysis.
Secondary sources are cited without interrogating their authority or perspective; include a brief evaluation of source reliability to strengthen analysis.
The discussion of Eugeneâs public versus private persona offers a nuanced contrast that deepens the critical evaluation of his contradictory worldview.
The claim that âcivilizedâ is oxymoronic requires further theoretical support or citation; provide a source or elaboration for this judgement.
The line âEverything I do for youâ¦â is characterized as manipulative without considering potential narrative bias; acknowledge complexity by discussing alternative readings.
The title page clearly states the investigationâs title and research question, establishing focus immediately and framing the study effectively against the IB English A criteria.
Repetitive recounting of silent rituals hinders conciseness; synthesize evidence and focus on the strongest examples to maintain analytical momentum.
The term âdialogueâ is misapplied to internal monologue here; use precise literary terminology to describe narrative techniques accurately.
Inclusion of word count and subject details on the title page meets IB formatting requirements and demonstrates attention to administrative conventions.
The âScope of researchâ outlines sources but does not explain why each was chosen; include justification of selection criteria to strengthen methodology.
The analysis of silence often summarizes plot events rather than interrogating its symbolic function; deepen critical commentary on how silence supports your thesis.
Long, dense paragraphs on colonial paradox impede flow; break discussions into focused sub-points to enhance readability.
Primary quotations illustrating hypocrisy are well-chosen and enrich the critical argument about colonial and familial contradictions.
This section would benefit from integrating secondary scholarship on familial manipulation to substantiate claims and situate analysis in academic discourse.
Use of clear subheadings in the conclusion enhances navigation and underscores each component of the summative argument.
The vivid personification of laughter vividly contrasts the oppressive atmosphere, demonstrating skilled use of literary concepts.
The author name is misspelled as âAdicheâ in multiple instances; ensure consistent spelling of proper nouns to maintain professionalism.
The âWhat has been achievedâ section provides a concise summary that effectively synthesizes key findings of the investigation.
Citation style is inconsistent here (parenthetical citations sometimes omit âp.â); standardize referencing to meet IB formatting conventions.
The figurines metaphor for psychological fragility is original and well-developed, enriching the discussion of domestic violence.
The Limitations paragraph notes time constraints but does not reflect on how these impacted the depth of analysis; elaborate this for stronger critical engagement.
Bibliography entries are not ordered alphabetically by author surname; reorder the list to comply with standard IB presentation rules.
